articles.title: China’s Aiming for the Moon, and NASA Is Looking Over Its Shoulder
articles.summary: The U.S. space agency launched a lunar flyby Wednesday, but Beijing is pursuing its own space program with formidable focus. Here’s what we know about it, in photos and videos.

articles.title: 25 years ago today, a mid-air collision sparked an 11-day U.S.–China standoff
articles.summary: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.

articles.title: Iran continues strikes across the Persian Gulf despite Trump's warning
articles.summary: Iran continued to target Gulf countries with ballistic missiles and drones Thursday as the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad issued a security alert warning of attacks by Iran-backed militias.

articles.title: Trump Says Strait of Hormuz Is Not America’s Problem, and How Musk May Get Richer
articles.summary: Plus, does every middle schooler really need a laptop?

articles.title: As hoops betting spikes, it's New Hampshire and other states vs. prediction markets
articles.summary: In New Hampshire and states with legalized sports gambling, wagering helps fund government services. But now competitors like Kalshi and Polymarket are getting a cut of the action.

articles.title: Every Trump Threat to Abandon NATO Hollows It Out
articles.summary: Doubts that the United States would come to the aid of NATO allies increase each time, prompting Europeans to consider an alliance without Washington.

articles.title: 5 Takeaways From Trump’s Address on Iran
articles.summary: President Trump did not define a clear path out of the conflict, which he estimated would end within three weeks.

articles.title: Have Trump's tariffs worked? This is where things stand a year after 'Liberation Day'
articles.summary: It has been a year since President Trump announced double-digit tariffs on imports from around the world. So far, those levies have not produced the economic boom the president promised.

articles.title: In a thunderous launch, Artemis II astronauts leave Earth. Here's what's next
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. Here's what to expect over their roughly 10-day journey.

articles.title: Trump's VA killed a home loan program. Vets are now losing their homes because of it
articles.summary: Foreclosures on VA loans are at their highest level in a decade. VA has a fix but it is months away and could still leave vets worse off than most other homeowners.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Trump says war in Iran is 'nearing completion' in national address, Iranian officials react to President Trump's speech on Iran war, SCOTUS hears arguments on birthright citizenship.

articles.title: Pakistan is playing intermediary in the Iran war, a role it has played before
articles.summary: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.

articles.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez
articles.summary: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.

articles.title: Artemis II Completes First Day of Its NASA Lunar Mission
articles.summary: The crew, three Americans and a Canadian, are the first humans to travel to the moon in more than 50 years. They will not land on the surface, but the mission will pave the way for future visits.

articles.title: Trump Has Discussed Firing Attorney General Pam Bondi
articles.summary: President Trump has not made a final decision, but he has floated the idea of replacing Ms. Bondi with Lee Zeldin, the E.P.A. administrator.

articles.title: Bondi Is Vulnerable as Republican Frustrations Over DOJ’s Epstein Files Missteps Grow
articles.summary: Ms. Bondi’s critics inside and outside the administration say she has made unforced errors that have turned the Justice Department’s handling of the Epstein files into a political crisis.

articles.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
articles.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.

articles.title: Is the U.S. Navy ready to clear sea mines in the Persian Gulf?
articles.summary: Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.

articles.title: "Lives will be lost": How the U.K.'s aid cuts may affect parts of Africa
